skill.name: fetch-url
skill.description: Render a web page, extract the main readable content, and return Markdown or another supported output format.
skill.summary: Fetches a URL with Playwright and converts rendered page content into cleaner text or Markdown.
skill.author: DCjanus

security_audit.summary: Static network findings are true positives because the skill intentionally opens user supplied HTTP and HTTPS URLs with Playwright. The command and weak cryptography alerts in SKILL.md are mostly false positives from documentation examples and Markdown text, but the executable script, browser rendering, and user selected output file create medium operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Headless Browser Opens User Supplied URLs
medium_findings.description: The script accepts a URL argument, only restricts the scheme to HTTP or HTTPS, and passes it to Playwright navigation. This is expected for the skill, but it can reach internal or untrusted sites from the user's machine and execute page JavaScript in a browser context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit and central to the skill. The risk is medium because it is user directed, but there are no host allowlists or private network protections.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands Are Expected Usage
low_findings.description: SKILL.md shows commands for running the local script and installing Playwright Chromium. These are user-facing setup and invocation examples, not hidden command execution inside the skill instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are in documentation blocks and refer to the skill's own script. They do not include obfuscated commands, command substitution, or untrusted interpolation.

low_findings.title: User Selected Output Path Can Write Files
low_findings.description: The optional output argument writes extracted content to the provided file path. This is normal CLI behavior, but users should avoid overwriting important files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is direct and visible in the code. The risk is low because the path is supplied by the user and no automatic sensitive path is targeted.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged description line discusses Markdown output and does not contain cryptographic logic. No weak hashing or encryption use was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is front matter text, not executable code. The script imports no cryptographic module and performs no cryptographic operation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Navigation To Arbitrary HTTP Or HTTPS URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses Playwright page navigation against the supplied URL after checking only the scheme. This can expose the runtime network location to untrusted web pages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present and semantically required for the skill. The impact depends on what URLs users choose and what network the tool runs from.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Executable Script Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to run its local script directly. This is normal for a CLI helper, but marketplace users should treat community scripts as executable code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The executable script and invocation instruction are explicit. The code appears aligned with the stated purpose, so this is an operational risk rather than evidence of malware.

content.user_title: Extract Clean Content From Web Pages
content.value_statement: Web pages often include scripts, menus, ads, and layout text that waste context. This skill renders a URL and returns cleaner main content in Markdown or another supported format.
actual_capabilities: Runs a local Python script with uv script dependencies.
Renders HTTP and HTTPS pages with a Chromium browser through Playwright.
Extracts main page content with trafilatura.
Outputs Markdown by default and supports text, HTML, JSON, XML, CSV, and TEI XML formats.
Can write extracted content to a user specified output file.
Can use an auto detected or user specified Chromium based browser path.
limitations: It requires uv, Python 3.14 or newer, and Playwright compatible browser support.
It only accepts HTTP and HTTPS URLs.
Extraction can fail when a page blocks automation or lacks readable main content.
Raw HTML output may include page noise and untrusted page content.

use_cases.title: Collect Article Text For Research
use_cases.description: Render a source page and capture its main article content as Markdown for later review.

use_cases.title: Prepare Web Content For AI Review
use_cases.description: Reduce page noise before asking Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to summarize or compare sources.

use_cases.title: Archive Rendered Page Content
use_cases.description: Save extracted content from dynamic pages into a local file for documentation or audit notes.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch One Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fetch-url to extract the main content from this page as Markdown: https://example.com

prompt_templates.title: Save A Markdown Copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fetch-url to render this URL and save the cleaned Markdown to ./notes/page.md: https://example.com

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch these two URLs as Markdown, then compare their main claims and list any missing source details.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Rendered HTML
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fetch-url with raw HTML output for this page, then identify whether the important content is present after rendering.

output_examples.input: Fetch a news article URL as Markdown.
output_examples.output: A cleaned Markdown version of the article with headings, links, and main body text preserved.

output_examples.input: Render a documentation page and save it to a file.
output_examples.output: A local Markdown file containing the extracted page content, plus a console message showing the saved path.

output_examples.input: Request raw HTML output for a dynamic page.
output_examples.output: The rendered page HTML is returned without trafilatura cleanup for manual inspection.

best_practices: Run the skill only against URLs you trust or need to inspect.
Use Markdown output first because it removes much of the page noise.
Write output to a dedicated notes folder to avoid overwriting important files.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to browse private internal sites unless you understand the network exposure.
Do not treat extracted text as verified or safe without reviewing the source.
Do not use raw HTML output when you only need article text.

faq.question: Does this skill access the network?
faq.answer: Yes. It opens the URL you provide with a local Playwright Chromium browser.

faq.question: Can it fetch local files?
faq.answer: No. The script accepts only HTTP and HTTPS URL schemes.

faq.question: What is the default output format?
faq.answer: Markdown is the default output format.

faq.question: Can it save output to disk?
faq.answer: Yes. The output option writes the extracted content to the path you provide.

faq.question: Why might extraction fail?
faq.answer: A site may block automation, take too long to load, or lack clear main article content.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is suitable for publication with a medium risk warning about network access and local script execution.
